title: Exit methods should not be called
description: Exit methods should not be calledAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellcwesuspiciousWhy is this an issue?CallingEnvironment.Exit(exitCode)orApplication.Exit()terminates the process and returns an exit code to the operating system..Each of these methods should be used with extreme care, and only when the intent is to stop the whole application.Noncompliant code exampleEnvironment.Exit(0); Application.Exit();ExceptionsThese methods are ignored insideMain.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since9.1Analyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|C
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Environment.Exit() and Application.Exit() should not be called except in Main method

logic.reasoning: Detects calls to Environment.Exit() or Application.Exit() which terminate the entire application. These should only be used in Main method. Pattern matches method calls in MethodDeclaration nodes and excludes Main method variations and unit tests. Note: Constructor, property, and finalizer usage is not detected due to scanner framework limitations.

should_not_trigger: public void ProcessData() { throw new Exception(); }

logic.self_critique_notes: Pattern detects exit method calls and excludes Main method. Edge case: Main method with different signatures may need additional patterns.
